question: How is the capital recovery factor (A/P, I, n) related to the uniform series sinking fund factor (A/F, i, n)? I is the effective annual rate of return, and n is the number periods.

options.d: (A/P, i, n) = (A/F, i, n) + i / n

question: What is annuity?

options.a: the future worth of a present amount
options.b: an annual repayment of a loan
options.c: a series of uniform amounts over a period of time
options.d: a lump sum at the end pf the year

correct_answer_text: a series of uniform amounts over a period of time

question: What is the most nearly the effective annual interest rate on a loan if the nominal interest rate is 12% /yr compounded quarterly?

A company has $100,000 to spend on the various projects listed. Using these projects only, what should this company consider its minimum attractive rate of return to be? Project A ($10k, 14%), B ($25k, 10%), C ($50k, 12%), D ($40k, 16%), E ($25k, 11%), F ($30k, 10%), G ($20k, 12%)
What is most nearly the internal rate of return on the following cash flow? t=0 spend $100,000; t=1 spend $50,000; t=2 receive $100,000; t=3 receive $103,000.

A steel drum manufacturer incurs a yearly fixed operating cost of $200,000. each drum manufactured costs $160 to produce and sells for $200. what is the manufacturer's break-even sales volume in drums per year?

XYZ Corporation manufacturers bookcases that it sells for $65 each. It costs XYZ $35,000/yr to operate its plant. If the cost to produce one bookcase is $50, how many bookcases must be sold each year for XYZ to avoid taking a loss?

You and 3 friends are each downloading a different game simultaneously over a shared 500 Mbps fiber internet connection. File sizes are You (90 GB), Friend A (60 GB), Friend B (30 GB), Friend C (30 GB). Approximately how long (in minutes) will it take you to finish downloading your 90 GB game?

A manufacturing firm maintains one assembly line to produce signal generators. Weekly demand for the generators is 35 units, and the line operates for 7 h/d, 5 d/wk. What is the maximum production time per unit required of the line in order to meet demand?
A volume of water is subjected to an increase in pressure of 1.1 MPa, resulting in a volume decrease of 0.05%. Estimate the speed of sound in water.
Which of the following events will cause the optimal lot size, given by the classic EOQ model with no shortages, to increase?

A manufacturer of sports equipment produces tennis rackets for which there is a demand of 200/mo. The production setup cost for each batch of rackets is $300. in addition, the inventory carrying cost for each racket is $24/yr. Using the EOQ model, which is most nearly the best production batch size for the rackets?

The internal rate of return of a project that involves an initial investment with subsequent positive cash flows is 18%. Given MARRs for Companies A (12%), B (15%), C (18%), D (19%), which company will be most likely to accept the project?
What type of fire extinguisher is most suitable for electrical fires?
Which of the following is NOT part of the fire triangle?
What is the indirect product cost (IPC) spending variance?

options.a: the difference between actual IPC and IPC absorbed
options.b: the difference between actual IPC and IPC volume-adjusted budget
options.c: the IPC volume-adjusted budget ((fixed + (volume)(variable IPC rate))
options.d: the IPC volume-adjusted budget minus the total IPC absorbed

correct_answer_text: the difference between actual IPC and IPC volume-adjusted budget

question: How is the material purchase price variance defined?
Which of the following does NOT affect owner's equity?

options.d: expense to get license to start operation

question: Higrow Company is planning to grow 30% during the next fiscal year. What has to increase if Higrow is to achieve their goal?

options.a: the ration of sales to total assets
options.b: the ratio of total assets to equity
options.d: any combination of a, b, and c

correct_answer_text: any combination of a, b, and c

question: Tops Corporation's gross margin is 45% of sales. Operating expenses such as sales and administration are 15% of sales. Tops is in a 40% tax bracket. What percent of sales is their profit after taxes?

Calculate the acid test ratio for a company with assets: cash ($10,000), receivables ($12,000), inventory ($7,000), capital equipment ($20,000); and liabilities: payables ($17,000), notes due ($6,000), long term debt ($3,000).
What is annuity?

options.a: A series of uniform amounts over a period of time
options.b: The future worth of a present amount
options.c: An annual repayment of a loan
options.d: A lump sum at the end of a year

correct_answer_text: A series of uniform amounts over a period of time

question: When using the net present worth calculations to compare two project which of the following could invalidate the calculation?

options.d: difference in the magnitude of the project

question: What most investments with the same present worth and unequal lives have?

options.d: identical equivalent uniform annual cash flows

question: What is work process classified as?
Which of the following does not affect owner's equity?
What is acid test ratio?

options.a: the ratio of current assets (exclusive of inventory) to the total current liabilities
options.b: the ratio of owner's equity to total current liabilities
options.c: the ratio of assets to total liabilities
options.d: the ratio of profit after taxes to equity

correct_answer_text: the ratio of current assets (exclusive of inventory) to the total current liabilities

question: It is responsible for the procurement and administration of funds with the view of achieving the objective of business.
Funds supplied and used by owners of an enterprise in the expectations that a profit will be earned.
Funds that are required to make the enterprises or project a going concern.
The length of time usually in years, for the cumulative annual profit to equalized the initial investment is.
The unrecovered depreciation which resulted from poor estimate of the life of equipment.
What factor must be considered by investor before he invests?
A market situation with only one buyer of an item and no goods substitute.
A market situation with few sellers and many buyers.
It is an aggregation of individuals formed for the purpose of conducting a business and recognized by law as fictitious person.
It is the secondary book of accounts of an enterprise where the credit and debit of an enterprise were written.
It is the original book of accounts of an enterprise.
It is the reduction in the level of national income and output usually accompanied by the fall in general price level.
It is the decrease in money's purchasing power due to increase in material cost and services.
It is the decrease in worth of the property due to passage of time.
It is the difference between the present worth and the future worth of negotiable paper.
It is amount earned by one unit of principal during a unit of time.
It pertains to the series of equal payments occurring at equal period of interval of time.
It is a method of paying debt including the principal and interest which is usually done in a series of equal payments occurring at equal period of interval of time.
It is type of annuity where payments are made indefinitely or forever.
It pertains series of payments on equal interval of time with common difference.
It pertains series of payments on equal interval of time with common ratio.
It is the sum of the first cost and the present worth of annual maintenance and operational costs, costs of repair and the renewal costs.
It is the measure of the effectiveness of an investment.
